title: Por y Para — Lecciones with Jennifer
description: Por vs para handout: a brief rule summary, fill-in and multiple-choice drills, a short analysis text, and written production.

lessons.summary: Jennifer's rule. Use PARA for: destino (destination), objetivo o finalidad (purpose/goal), fecha límite (deadline), and opinión. Use POR for: causa o razón (cause/reason), medio (means), intercambio (exchange), movimiento por un lugar (movement through a place), tiempo aproximado (approximate time), and agente (the doer, in passive sentences). Shorthand: PARA looks ahead to a goal or endpoint; POR looks back to the cause or the means.

examples.spanish: Salí de casa por la mañana para ir a la oficina.
examples.english: I left home in the morning to go to the office.
examples.note: por = approximate time; para = purpose — both in one sentence

examples.spanish: Compré una pulsera por diez euros.
examples.english: I bought a bracelet for ten euros.
I arrived late because of the traffic.

examples.spanish: Entregué el informe para las tres.
examples.english: I handed in the report by three o'clock.

examples.spanish: Este regalo es para tu cumpleaños.
examples.english: This gift is for your birthday.

items.promptEnglish: This coffee is for you.
items.explanation: PARA marks the destination — the person the coffee is intended for.

items.promptEnglish: Thanks for the help.
items.explanation: POR gives the cause/reason for the thanks.

items.promptEnglish: We walked along the beach at sunset.
items.explanation: POR expresses movement through/along a place.

items.promptEnglish: I need the report by Thursday.
items.explanation: PARA marks a deadline (fecha límite).

items.prompt: Viajamos a Chile ___ ver a mi familia.
items.promptEnglish: We travel to Chile to see my family.
items.explanation: PARA + infinitive expresses purpose (finalidad).

items.prompt: Te envío los documentos ___ correo electrónico.
items.promptEnglish: I'm sending you the documents by email.
items.explanation: POR marks the means (medio) by which something is done.

items.promptEnglish: I missed the train because I arrived late.
items.explanation: POR gives the cause. POR + infinitive = 'because of (doing) something'.

items.prompt: Este libro es ___ estudiar para el examen.
items.promptEnglish: This book is for studying for the exam.
items.explanation: PARA + infinitive expresses purpose. (Note the second 'para el examen' = purpose again.)

items.promptEnglish: Shall we walk around the centre today?
items.explanation: POR expresses movement through a place — walking around/through the centre.

items.promptEnglish: This gift is for your birthday.
items.explanation: PARA marks the destination/occasion the gift is meant for.

items.promptEnglish: I couldn't go because of the rain.
items.explanation: POR marks the cause — the rain is the reason.

items.promptEnglish: I work hard to achieve my goals.
items.explanation: PARA + infinitive expresses purpose (finalidad).

items.prompt: Te cambio mi turno ___ el tuyo.
items.promptEnglish: I'll swap my shift for yours.
items.explanation: POR marks an exchange (intercambio) — one thing traded for another.

items.prompt: Salí de casa ___ la mañana para ir a la oficina.
items.promptEnglish: I left home in the morning to go to the office.
items.explanation: POR is used for approximate time / part of the day: 'por la mañana'.

items.promptEnglish: I bought a bracelet for ten euros.
items.explanation: POR marks exchange — the price paid in return.

items.promptEnglish: I arrived late because of the traffic.
items.explanation: POR marks the cause — the traffic is the reason for arriving late.

items.promptEnglish: I handed in the report by three o'clock.
items.explanation: PARA marks a deadline (fecha límite) — the time by which it was done.

items.prompt: Rewrite stating the reason with POR: "No fui a la fiesta porque estaba enfermo." → "No fui a la fiesta ___ estar enfermo."
items.promptEnglish: I didn't go to the party because of being ill.
items.explanation: A cause can be expressed with POR + infinitive: 'por estar enfermo' = 'because (I was) ill'.

items.prompt: Escribe una frase con PARA que exprese finalidad o destino.
items.promptEnglish: Write a sentence with PARA expressing purpose or destination.
items.guidance: The sentence must be grammatical Spanish and use 'para' to express either purpose (para + infinitive, or para que + subjunctive) or a destination/recipient (e.g. 'Estudio para ser ingeniero' or 'Este libro es para María'). Mark incorrect if it uses 'por', or uses 'para' for a meaning that should take 'por' (cause, exchange, means, movement).
items.explanation: PARA marks purpose (finalidad) and destination/recipient — where something is headed or what it's for.

items.prompt: Escribe una frase con POR que exprese causa o medio.
items.promptEnglish: Write a sentence with POR expressing cause or means.
items.guidance: The sentence must be grammatical Spanish and use 'por' to express either a cause/reason (e.g. 'Cerraron la calle por la nieve') or a means (e.g. 'Hablamos por teléfono'). Mark incorrect if it uses 'para', or uses 'por' for a meaning that should take 'para' (purpose, destination, deadline).
items.sampleAnswer: Te llamo por teléfono esta noche.
items.explanation: POR marks cause (the reason something happens) and means (the medium through which it happens).
